Text:
One single continuous photograph — no panels, grids, or split frames.

Use the uploaded photo as the exact base composition. The street in the source
is empty — populate it with the people and vehicles specified below. Remove all
modern businesses, logos, brands, storefront names, banners, advertisements, and
signage visible in the source image.

PRESERVE (must match source exactly)
- camera: eye-level, street-facing, fov 80
- main street road, 2-lane, asphalt
- sidewalks present, curbs present
- driveways: alley entrance mid-block; parking: parallel street parking both sides
- two-story brick commercial building at left block face, 2 stories, red brick, cast iron storefront columns, flat parapet roof, at sidewalk setback
- single-story retail building at center block face, 1 story, brick, plate glass storefront, flat with low parapet roof, at sidewalk setback
- corner mixed-use building at right corner, 3 stories, brick, stone trim cornice, flat roof, at sidewalk setback
- utilities: overhead power and telephone lines, wooden utility poles every 50 feet
- landscape: concrete sidewalks, brick pavers at crosswalks, small planted tree pits
- immutable elements: cast iron storefront columns on left building, recessed corner entrance on right building, brick alley mid-block
Keep this location instantly recognizable.

OUTPUT FORMAT
Create a TRUE 9:16 vertical portrait image — one single frame. Expand the SAME
scene naturally above (sky) and below (street/pavement) — this is canvas
extension of one photograph, not additional panels. Do not stretch or distort
the source geometry.

TRANSFORM TO 1995
Clinton era prosperity. A 1995 main street — the video rental store at its absolute peak, a computer shop newly arrived, chain logos displacing the last hand-painted signs.

CONDITION: thriving — well-maintained, freshly painted, active business, clean lot

PERIOD DETAILS
- pizza and sub shop with a '2 SLICES + SODA $2.99' sign in the window
- RED ROBIN DINER with 'HOT COFFEE 75¢' in the window
- barber shop with its spinning barber pole, unchanged
- window signs: 'SUB COMBO $3.99', 'FREE INTERNET TRIAL — 30 DAYS'
- a wall-mounted payphone or payphone booth
- a cluster of coin-operated newspaper boxes on the corner
- a bench with a local realtor ad
Typography: clean geometric sans-serif, early digital influence.
Sign text is only what appears in quotes — do not turn other words from this prompt into signage.

PEOPLE
EXACTLY 11 people TOTAL: 4 near sidewalk (largest, foreground), 4 opposite sidewalk mid-block, 3 distant, far end of block. Grouped in pairs, threes, and singles; sidewalks busy, driving lanes clear.
- a man walking a dog past the computer shop
- a woman talking on the wall-mounted payphone
- a mom loading groceries into a minivan
Clothing: men in backwards caps, baggy jeans; women in slip dresses, chokers. Fashion palette: forest green, maroon, mustard yellow. No posing or eye contact. All people on sidewalks, at storefronts, or beside parked vehicles — the driving lanes stay empty of pedestrians.

VEHICLES
EXACTLY 6 period vehicles, all different:
- 1991-1995 Dodge Caravan — rounded second-gen minivan — black
- 1995-1999 Dodge Neon — small rounded economy, friendly face — white
- 1992-1995 Pontiac Grand Am — compact with ribbed plastic cladding — hunter green
- 1992-1997 Ford Taurus — rounded jellybean shape, oval theme — navy blue
- 1989-1997 Geo Metro — very small economy hatchback — burgundy
- 1994-1997 Honda Accord — smooth rounded sedan — silver
Parked with gaps; no vehicle newer than 1995.
All vehicles obey normal US right-hand traffic flow — parked parallel to the curb, each facing the same direction as its adjacent lane. Nothing sideways, diagonal, or against traffic.
At least one full driving lane in each direction stays clear of parked vehicles, meters, and curb infrastructure — through traffic is never blocked.
PLACEMENT: three parked on the RIGHT side; the rest parked on the LEFT side. No vehicle in the same spot as any other era.

ENVIRONMENT
- road markings: yellow center line, white edge lines, turn lane arrows
- utilities: overhead power lines standard, cable TV and early phone data lines on poles

TREES
- oak tree at sidewalk left near corner: established tree, modest canopy
- maple tree at sidewalk center in front of retail: very young sapling
Tree sizes MUST follow this specification even where they differ from the source photo.

PHOTOGRAPHIC STYLE
COLOR photograph — Kodak Gold 200 or Fujifilm Superia 200 look.
Color: neutral to slightly warm tones. Grain: fine grain, cleaner than 1985.
Photorealistic — NOT black-and-white, no HDR.

PRIVACY
Blur all faces and license plates.
SelectedVehicles: 1995-1999 Dodge Neon — small rounded economy, friendly face
1992-1995 Pontiac Grand Am — compact with ribbed plastic cladding
1992-1997 Ford Taurus — rounded jellybean shape, oval theme
1989-1997 Geo Metro — very small economy hatchback
